message:
fix: UTF-8 boundary panics in snippets, index-out-of-bounds in render, underflow in screen scroll

Three independent crash-path fixes:

tantivy_query.rs:
- Fix panic when highlight position falls on a multi-byte UTF-8 char
  boundary in extract_snippets(). Previously, slicing raw_fragment at
  relative_pos and relative_pos+match_len could land in the middle of
  a multi-byte character, causing a panic on string slice.
  Now clamps positions to fragment length, then adjusts to the nearest
  valid char boundary using char_indices() — scanning backward for the
  start position and forward for the end position.

termwindow/render/mod.rs:
- Replace cluster[0] and shape_info[0] indexing with .first().ok_or_else()
  in lock indicator glyph rendering. If the lock icon character produces
  an empty cluster or shaping returns no glyphs (e.g., missing font glyph),
  this now returns a descriptive error instead of panicking with
  index-out-of-bounds.

term/screen.rs:
- Use saturating_sub(self.physical_rows) instead of bare subtraction in
  scrollback_or_visible_row() and erase_scrollback(). If lines.len() is
  less than physical_rows (can happen during terminal resize or
  initialization), the subtraction would underflow on usize, wrapping to
  a huge value and causing subsequent out-of-bounds access or mass
  line deletion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
